questions.question: Which creature is the largest mammal to have ever existed on Earth?
How many wings does a honey bee have?
Which is the only mammal capable of true powered flight?
What is the collective noun used for a group of lions?
A cheetah can reach its top speed of approximately 70 mph in how many seconds?
How many hearts does an octopus have?
In which animal species does the male carry the eggs and give birth to the young?
Which animal is the tallest living land animal in the world?
The platypus and the echidna are the only two mammals that do what?
What is a shark's skeleton made of?
Which of the following is considered the slowest mammal in the world?
Which is the only bird that can fly backwards?
What color is a polar bear's skin under its white fur?
Which of these is the largest land-dwelling carnivore?
In which country can you find Komodo dragons in the wild?
What is a baby kangaroo called?
What part of their body do butterflies use to taste?
What is the primary reason African elephants have such large ears?
The 'tusk' of a narwhal is actually which type of body part?
The tongue of a blue whale weighs about as much as which other animal?
What allows cats to see clearly in near-total darkness?
What percentage of a giant panda's diet consists of bamboo?
Which of these is the largest living primate in the world?
Reindeer are the only mammals whose eyes change color with the seasons; what color do they turn in winter?
Which bird possesses the largest wingspan of any living bird, measuring up to 11 feet?
How many arms can a sea star (starfish) lose and still fully regenerate?
How long can it take for a sloth to digest a single meal?
Which animal has fingerprints that are so similar to humans that they have been mistaken at crime scenes?
How many degrees can an owl rotate its head?
Which sea creature is considered the most venomous in the world?
What is the average gestation period for an African Elephant?
The axolotl, a salamander that retains its gills into adulthood, is native to only one country. Which one?
Where does a woodpecker's tongue wrap around to protect its brain from impact?
How many compartments does a cow's stomach have?
Which animal has the largest brain of any living species?
Where are the venomous spurs located on a male platypus?
What is the top recorded diving speed of a Peregrine Falcon?
Generally, how do moths hold their wings when at rest compared to butterflies?
What are the irritating hairs that some tarantulas flick at predators called?
Which animal has the longest migration of any known species, traveling from the Arctic to the Antarctic?
Why is the honey badger's skin so effective against predator attacks?
questions.answer: It is very loose and thick

questions.question: Pigeons are one of the few birds that produce a nutrient-rich substance to feed their young. What is it called?
An electric eel can generate a discharge of up to how many volts?
Despite their name, mountain goats are actually more closely related to which animal?
What is a rhinoceros horn primarily made of?
The Nine-banded Armadillo almost always gives birth to how many identical young?
Which of these traits is unique to the naked mole rat among rodents?
The Greater Honeyguide bird is famous for leading which mammal to beehives so they can share the spoils?
Wombats are the only animals in the world known to produce droppings in what distinct shape?
Which elusive deep-sea creature is the largest known invertebrate on Earth?
Snow leopards are incredible jumpers; how far can they leap in a single bound?
What is the primary purpose of the bright blue tongue of the Blue-tongued Skink?
Chameleons can move their eyes independently, giving them a field of vision of how many degrees?
The Common Basilisk lizard is nicknamed the 'Jesus Christ Lizard' because it can do what?
What do male mosquitoes primarily eat?
How do sea otters typically open the hard shells of their prey like clams and mussels?
What specialized 'third eyelid' do falcons use to clear debris and moisten eyes during high-speed dives?
In which part of its body is a shrimp's heart located?
An ostrich kick is powerful enough to kill which major predator?
The Alaskan Wood Frog can survive the winter by doing what to its body?
Leafcutter ants do not eat the leaves they cut; instead, they use them to grow what food source?
What color is a giraffe's tongue, likely to protect it from sunburn while eating?
What happens to a scorpion when it is exposed to ultraviolet (UV) light?
What is the name of the deadly neurotoxin found in pufferfish (Fugu)?
Which land animal is the closest living relative to the manatee?
The Superb Lyrebird is famous for its ability to mimic which of the following sounds?
Just as humans have unique fingerprints, gorillas have unique what?
What is the primary, albeit gross, defense mechanism of a Turkey Vulture when threatened?
On average, how many hours a day does a male lion spend sleeping or resting?
How do clownfish survive living among the stinging tentacles of sea anemones?
questions.answer: They have a protective mucus layer

questions.question: The Mantis Shrimp can strike its prey with the same acceleration as which of the following?
The heart of a Blue Whale is roughly the size of which object?
Since they do not have nipples, how do female platypuses provide milk to their young?
What is the name of the dance honey bees use to tell their hive mates where to find flowers?
What color does the Arctic Fox's coat change to during the summer months?
Which continent is the only one on Earth with no native ant species?
How quickly can a cuttlefish change its skin color and texture to match its surroundings?
How many neck vertebrae does a giraffe have (the same number as a human)?
Where are a starfish's eyes located?
questions.answer: At the tip of each arm

questions.question: What can a hummingbird's heart rate reach during flight?
Approximately how many muscles are in an elephant's trunk?
What do pigeons use to help them navigate back home over long distances?
Which species is the largest living lizard in the world?
A woodpecker's tongue is typically how many times longer than its bill?
What extreme defense mechanism does a sea cucumber use to scare away predators?
Unlike most birds which have three or four toes, how many toes does an ostrich have on each foot?
How many muscles does a cat have in each ear to allow for precise rotation?
Historically, what has 'guano' (bat droppings) been highly valued for?
How deep can a sperm whale dive in search of giant squid?
What is the record lifespan for a queen ant in a laboratory setting?
What is the shell of an armadillo primarily made of?
What color is the 'blood sweat' secreted by hippos that acts as a natural sunscreen?
From how far away can the roar of a Howler Monkey be heard through the jungle?
Where does the Slow Loris produce the toxin that makes it the world's only venomous primate?
Weight for weight, spider silk is stronger than which material?
What is a group of lions commonly known as?
Which animal is renowned for its ability to change its skin color to match its environment?
What is the fastest land animal on Earth?
Which of these marine animals is classified as a mammal?
What is the only mammal naturally capable of sustained flight?
